annotations.label: Length of an interval

anchor.snippet: denote its length

annotations.note: The length of an interval is its Lebesgue measure, e.g. volume (Icc a b) = ofReal (b - a).

annotations.label: Lebesgue outer measure (one dimension)

anchor.snippet: is defined as an infimum

annotations.note: The Lebesgue–Stieltjes outer measure (ofFunction over interval lengths) generalizes the 1-D Lebesgue outer measure, but there is no dedicated Lebesgue-outer-measure declaration.

annotations.label: Lebesgue outer measure in higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: For any rectangular cuboid

annotations.note: Mathlib builds n-dimensional volume as a product/Haar measure, not as an outer measure constructed via infima over cuboid covers.

annotations.label: Carathéodory criterion

anchor.snippet: satisfies the Carathéodory criterion whenever

annotations.note: IsCaratheodory m s states m t = m (t ∩ s) + m (t \ s) for all t, exactly the Carathéodory criterion.

annotations.label: Lebesgue-measurable set

anchor.snippet: are said to be Lebesgue-measurable

annotations.note: The Carathéodory σ-algebra of an outer measure is defined generically; Mathlib's volume uses the Borel σ-algebra rather than a specialized Lebesgue-measurable σ-algebra.

annotations.label: Measurable sets form a σ-algebra

anchor.snippet: forms a σ -algebra

annotations.note: OuterMeasure.caratheodory produces a MeasurableSpace (σ-algebra) of Carathéodory-measurable sets.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: is defined as its Lebesgue outer measure

annotations.note: Real.measureSpace gives the volume measure on ℝ, which is the Lebesgue measure (constructed as additive Haar measure).

annotations.label: Non-measurable sets exist in ZFC

anchor.snippet: ZFC proves that non-measurable sets do exist

annotations.note: Mathlib does not construct or prove the existence of a non-Lebesgue-measurable set.

annotations.label: Measure of a closed interval

anchor.snippet: Any closed interval

annotations.note: volume (Icc a b) = ENNReal.ofReal (b - a).

annotations.label: Open interval has same measure as closed

anchor.snippet: the difference between the two sets

annotations.note: volume (Ioo a b) = ofReal (b - a) equals volume (Icc a b), since the endpoints have measure zero (NoAtoms).

annotations.label: Measure of a Cartesian product of intervals

anchor.snippet: Any Cartesian product of intervals

annotations.note: volume (Icc a b) = ∏ i, ENNReal.ofReal (b i - a i) for the product box in ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Every Borel set is Lebesgue-measurable

anchor.snippet: every Borel set is Lebesgue-measurable

annotations.note: Every measurable (Borel) set is null-measurable, i.e. measurable for the completed (Lebesgue) measure.

annotations.label: Measurable sets that are not Borel

anchor.snippet: there are Lebesgue-measurable sets which are not Borel sets

annotations.note: Mathlib does not exhibit a Lebesgue-measurable set that is not Borel.

annotations.label: Countable set has measure zero

anchor.snippet: Any countable set of real numbers has Lebesgue measure 0

annotations.note: Set.Countable.measure_zero applies to volume on ℝ since it has the NoAtoms instance.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers have measure zero

anchor.snippet: the set of algebraic numbers is 0

annotations.note: Countability of the algebraic numbers is formalized; combined with Set.Countable.measure_zero it gives the result, but the measure-zero statement itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Cantor set and Liouville numbers have measure zero

anchor.snippet: The Cantor set and the set of Liouville numbers

annotations.note: The Liouville numbers' measure-zero is formalized (volume_setOf_liouville); the Cantor set is defined (cantorSet) but its measure-zero is not proved.

annotations.label: Axiom of determinacy implies all sets measurable

anchor.snippet: If the axiom of determinacy holds

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the axiom of determinacy implying universal Lebesgue measurability.

annotations.label: Vitali sets are non-measurable

anchor.snippet: Vitali sets are examples of sets that are not measurable

annotations.note: Mathlib does not construct Vitali sets or prove them non-measurable (the 'Vitali' files concern Vitali covering theorems).

annotations.label: Osgood curves have positive measure

anchor.snippet: Osgood curves are simple plane curves with positive Lebesgue measure

annotations.note: Osgood curves and their positive planar measure are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: A line in R^n has measure zero

anchor.snippet: Any line in

annotations.note: A line in ℝⁿ (n ≥ 2) is a proper affine subspace, which has zero Haar/Lebesgue measure by addHaar_affineSubspace.

annotations.label: Proper hyperplane has measure zero

anchor.snippet: every proper hyperplane has a zero Lebesgue measure

annotations.note: A proper hyperplane is a proper affine subspace, so addHaar_affineSubspace (or addHaar_submodule) gives it measure zero.

annotations.label: Volume of an n-ball

anchor.snippet: The volume of an n -ball can be calculated in terms of Euler's gamma function

annotations.note: volume (ball x r) = (ofReal r)^n * ofReal (√π^n / Γ(n/2 + 1)), the gamma-function formula for the n-ball volume.

annotations.label: Cartesian product of intervals is measurable

anchor.snippet: is a cartesian product of intervals

annotations.note: Each factor Icc is measurable (measurableSet_Icc), so a product box is measurable via MeasurableSet.pi.

annotations.label: Countable additivity

anchor.snippet: is a union of countably many pairwise disjoint

annotations.note: measure_iUnion gives μ (⋃ i, f i) = ∑' i, μ (f i) for a countable pairwise-disjoint measurable family.

annotations.label: Complement of a measurable set is measurable

anchor.snippet: then so is its complement

annotations.note: MeasurableSet.compl: the complement of a measurable set is measurable.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of the measure

anchor.snippet: for every Lebesgue-measurable set

annotations.note: Measures are ℝ≥0∞-valued by construction, so non-negativity is automatic from the type.

annotations.label: Monotonicity

anchor.snippet: (A consequence of 2.)

annotations.note: measure_mono: s ⊆ t implies μ s ≤ μ t.

annotations.label: Closure under countable unions and intersections

anchor.snippet: Countable unions and intersections of Lebesgue-measurable sets

annotations.note: MeasurableSet.iUnion and MeasurableSet.iInter give closure under countable unions and intersections.

annotations.label: Open, closed, and Borel sets are measurable

anchor.snippet: is an open or closed subset of

annotations.note: IsOpen.measurableSet and IsClosed.measurableSet show open/closed (hence Borel) sets are measurable in a BorelSpace.

annotations.label: Squeeze between open and closed sets (regularity)

anchor.snippet: approximately open

annotations.note: Outer/inner regularity of volume gives open supersets and closed subsets approximating any measurable set in measure.

annotations.label: Squeeze between Gδ and Fσ sets

anchor.snippet: between a containing G δ set and a contained F σ

annotations.note: Open/closed regular approximations are formalized; the precise Gδ-containing/Fσ-contained squeeze of equal measure is not a single named declaration.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure is a Radon measure

anchor.snippet: locally finite and inner regular

annotations.note: volume is locally finite (instance) and regular/inner-regular as a Haar measure, although Mathlib has no dedicated 'Radon measure' typeclass.

annotations.label: Strictly positive on open sets; full support

anchor.snippet: strictly positive on non-empty open sets

annotations.note: volume is an IsOpenPosMeasure (left-invariant + regular), so nonempty open sets get positive measure and the support is the whole space.

annotations.label: Completeness: subsets of null sets are null

anchor.snippet: A fortiori

annotations.note: measure_mono_null: any subset of a null set has measure zero.

annotations.label: Translation invariance

anchor.snippet: the translation of

annotations.note: volume is an additive Haar measure (IsAddLeftInvariant), so translation preserves measure.

annotations.label: Behavior under dilation

anchor.snippet: the dilation of

annotations.note: addHaar_smul: μ (r • s) = |r|^dim * μ s, the scaling of measure under dilation.

annotations.label: Behavior under linear transformations

anchor.snippet: is a linear transformation

annotations.note: addHaar_image_linearMap: a linear map scales measure by |det f|.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure is σ-finite

anchor.snippet: the property of being σ -finite

annotations.note: volume is a Haar measure on a σ-compact space, hence σ-finite.

annotations.label: Null set

anchor.snippet: is a null set if, for every

annotations.note: Null sets are formalized directly as sets with volume s = 0 (used pervasively via MeasureTheory.ae), without a separate ε-cover definition.

annotations.label: All countable sets are null

anchor.snippet: All countable sets are null sets

annotations.note: Set.Countable.measure_zero: countable sets are null for any NoAtoms measure, including volume.

annotations.label: Hausdorff dimension less than n implies null

anchor.snippet: has Hausdorff dimension less than n

annotations.note: measure_zero_of_dimH_lt: a set with Hausdorff dimension < d is null for any measure ≪ μH[d]; on ℝⁿ volume = μH[n].

annotations.label: Smith–Volterra–Cantor set

anchor.snippet: the Smith–Volterra–Cantor set

annotations.note: The Smith–Volterra–Cantor (fat Cantor) set is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Box in R^n and its volume

anchor.snippet: is a set of the form

annotations.note: BoxIntegral.Box is a product of intervals, and its volume equals ∏ i, (upper i - lower i) (BoxIntegral.volume_apply / Real.volume_Icc_pi).

annotations.label: Carathéodory's extension theorem

anchor.snippet: an application of Carathéodory's extension theorem

annotations.note: Carathéodory's extension restricting an outer measure to its Carathéodory-measurable σ-algebra is realized by OuterMeasure.toMeasure.

annotations.label: Non-measurable sets follow from the axiom of choice

anchor.snippet: is a consequence of the set-theoretical axiom of choice

annotations.note: Mathlib does not derive a non-measurable set from the axiom of choice.

annotations.label: Vitali theorem

anchor.snippet: The Vitali theorem , which follows from the axiom

annotations.note: Vitali's theorem on the existence of a non-measurable set is not formalized.

annotations.label: Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.snippet: such as those of the Banach–Tarski paradox

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solovay's independence result

anchor.snippet: Robert M. Solovay showed that

annotations.note: Solovay's model / independence result about universal measurability is not formalized.

annotations.label: Borel measure agrees with Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: agrees with the Lebesgue measure on those sets for which it is defined

annotations.note: Mathlib's volume is itself defined on the Borel σ-algebra, so it is the Borel measure; the Borel-vs-Lebesgue agreement is not separately stated.

annotations.label: More measurable sets than Borel sets

anchor.snippet: there are many more Lebesgue-measurable sets than there are Borel measurable sets

annotations.note: The cardinality comparison between Lebesgue-measurable and Borel sets is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure is a locally finite Borel measure

anchor.snippet: is automatically a locally finite Borel measure

annotations.note: volume on ℝ is defined on the Borel σ-algebra and carries the IsLocallyFiniteMeasure instance.

annotations.label: Borel measure translation-invariant but not complete

anchor.snippet: The Borel measure is translation-invariant, but not complete

annotations.note: Translation invariance of the Borel/volume measure is formalized; the non-completeness of the Borel measure is not stated.

annotations.label: Haar measure generalizes Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: can be defined on any locally compact group

annotations.note: haarMeasure is defined on any locally compact group; volume is realized as the additive Haar measure on ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Hausdorff measure generalizes Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: is a generalization of the Lebesgue measure that is useful

annotations.note: hausdorffMeasure_pi_real shows the n-dimensional Hausdorff measure equals Lebesgue volume on ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: No infinite-dimensional Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: there is no infinite-dimensional analogue of Lebesgue measure

annotations.note: The nonexistence of a translation-invariant locally-finite measure on infinite-dimensional spaces is not formalized.
